item.title: Teaching
item.description: As an Instructor at University of Pennsylvania STAT 4310 - Statistical Inference. Fall 2023. As an Instructor at Stanford University STATS 302 - Qualifying Exams Workshop (Theoretical Statistics). Summer 2018-2019. As a Teaching Assistant at Stanford University STATS 362 - Monte Carlo Methods. Winter 2019-2020. STATS 305 - Methods for Applied Statistics. Spring 2018-2019. STATS 200 - Introduction to Statistical Inference. Autumn 2018-2019, Winter 2017-2018, Winter 2016-2017, Autumn 2019-2020. STATS 237P - Theory of Investment Portfolios and Derivative Securities.

item.title: Talks
item.description: Knockoffs with side information. Seminar on Statistics and Data Science, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, March 2020. Knockoffs with side information. Bernoulli-IMS One World Symposium, August 2020. [video] [slides] Derandomizing knockoffs. International Seminar on Selective Inference, October 2020. [video] [slides] Dynamic batch learning in high-dimensional sparse linear contextual bandits. INFORMS Annual Meeting, October 2020. [slides] Dynamic batch learning in high-dimensional sparse linear contextual bandits. MOILS (Operation Management Lunch Seminar).

item.title: Research
item.description: Research interests Distribution-free inference Selective inference Data-driven decision making Causal inference Survival analysis Publications and Preprints Not all distributional shifts are equal: Fine-grained robust conformal inference [reproduction code] Jiahao Ai and Zhimei Ren Simultaneous activity and attenuation estimation in TOF-PET with TV-constrained nonconvex optimization [reproduction code] Zhimei Ren, Emil Y. Sidky, Rina Foygel Barber, Chien-Min Kao and Xiaochuan Pan IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging, 2024+ Iterative approximate cross-validation Yuetian Luo, Zhimei Ren and Rina Foygel Barber
